title: Support sequence intersect operator
body:
## Summary

This patch adds support for the `intersect` sequence operator (IEEE 1800-2023
16.9.6). `intersect` matches when both operand sequences match and have equal
length; if lengths differ, the match set is empty (constant-false).

## Reproducer

```systemverilog
module t (input clk);
  logic a, b, c, d;
  assert property (@(posedge clk) (a ##2 b) intersect (c ##2 d));
endmodule
```

On master:
```
%Error-UNSUPPORTED: t.sv:3:36: Unsupported: intersect (in sequence expression)
```

## Changes

- `verilog.y`: replace `BBUNSUP` with `AstSIntersect` node creation
- `V3AstNodeExpr.h`: add `AstSIntersect` (AstNodeBiop subclass)
- `V3Width.cpp`: handle `AstSIntersect` via `visit_log_and_or`, same as `AstSAnd`/`AstSOr`
- `V3AssertProp.cpp`: add `lowerSeqIntersect()` -- computes constant delay sums; equal lengths lower as `and`, unequal lengths replace with constant-false; pure boolean operands lower to `LogAnd`; `RangeDelayExpander` rejects `##[m:n]` operands (dynamic length prevents pairing)
- `V3AssertPre.cpp`: add `noSample(true)` to goto-rep counter variable, consistent with consecutive-rep counter
- `V3Dfg.cpp`, `V3DfgCse.cpp`: add `AstSIntersect` to defensive `UASSERT_OBJ` switch cases

## Test

- Added `test_regress/t/t_sequence_intersect.{v,py}` -- CRC-driven functional test: boolean intersect, same-length single-cycle and multi-cycle sequences, differing internal structure with equal total delay
- Added `test_regress/t/t_sequence_intersect_range_unsup.{v,py,out}` -- verifies `##[m:n]` operands produce UNSUPPORTED diagnostic

---
Developed by PlanV GmbH, assisted with Claude Code.

Reviewed by YilouWang.
